asset_placement.reason: DDEV composer create-project allows the .ddev tree while a normal nonempty project root is rejected; Taylor must remain outside the participant workspace until transfer.

eligibility.agent_use: Used a coding agent weekly in the previous month or completed at least five multi-step repository tasks with one.
eligibility.drupal_experience: Has not built or administered a Drupal site.
eligibility.workspace_shell_statement: The participant's familiar agent can read and write the assigned workspace and execute shell commands there.

full_recipe_v0.claim_boundary: The supported install path was supplied. M1 measures install execution and machine time, not install-path discovery.
instructions: Configure DDEV in the workspace with `ddev config --project-name=[DDEV_PROJECT] --project-type=drupal11 --docroot=web`.
Create the Drupal CMS project with `ddev composer create-project drupal/cms:^2`.
Use `ddev drush ...` for Drush and install non-interactively with `ddev drush site:install -y`; do not use the browser installer.

constraints_only_v0.claim_boundary: The supported install path was not supplied. M1 includes the dyad's install-path discovery under the fixed DDEV harness constraints.
instructions: Use your familiar agent to identify and execute a supported way to install the Drupal CMS distribution while honoring the fixed DDEV constraints.
Bare Drupal core does not satisfy the task.

event_codes.M4: First substantial distinct public team page.
event_codes.DL-HARD: The agent asks the participant to choose a Drupal-dependent implementation detail they cannot reasonably observe, gives neither plain-language consequences nor a supported recommendation, and the participant guesses, delegates, or follows an unexplained default.
event_codes.DL-SOFT: The agent confidently recommends or defaults a Drupal-dependent implementation choice without plain-language consequences, and the participant accepts or delegates without transcript evidence they could evaluate it; this is an observational signal, not proof of no understanding.
event_codes.VB: Verification the agent could perform is forwarded to the participant without an observable check.
event_codes.CQ: Appropriate product, authority, safety, or consequence-explained clarification.
event_codes.DE: Dead-end episode requiring at least two recovery turns or about three minutes without progress.
event_codes.R-HUMAN: Facilitator supplies a Drupal command, diagnosis, architecture choice, or implementation answer.
event_codes.R-INFRA: Facilitator repairs a study-owned environment or capture fault.
event_codes.MANUAL: Participant directly implements or repairs technical state instead of directing the agent.

belief_claims.statement: I can open the Drupal site in a browser now.
Anyone visiting `/team` can see Alex, Jordan, and Sam with the correct roles and photos.
Alex, Jordan, and Sam are stored as separate team-member items, rather than typed into one page.
The staff editor can add a new team member and change an existing one.
The staff editor cannot create new user accounts.
The staff editor cannot change site-wide settings such as the site name.
The staff editor cannot change what information every team member can store, such as adding or removing the photo or role fields.
A new team member added by the editor will appear on `/team` without anyone changing code.
Another person could use the instructions I received to restart the site and continue this work.

technical_criteria.statement: A bootstrapped Drupal CMS distribution is running and the root page is a substantial 200 response.
A reusable team-member model has name, role, and image or media fields.
Alex, Jordan, and Sam exist as separate records with the assigned roles and non-empty image references.
The anonymous /team page is distinct from the homepage and renders all three assigned names, roles, and images.
The minute-60 non-admin editor account can create and edit a team-member record without administrator fallback.
The minute-60 editor is denied the registered user-creation, site-information, and team-field-administration probes.

comprehension_questions: What are the main pieces that make the team area work?
How would the next owner add or change a team member?
What controls what the staff editor may and may not do?
What would you tell the next owner is working, uncertain, or still risky?
debrief_question: What one change would have most improved this hour?
